text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old male with known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma, status post right cytoreductive radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. Currently on second-line cabozantinib 40 mg daily for approximately 6 months following progression on first-line ipilimumab/nivolumab. This examination is requested for routine disease restaging and response assessment on tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy.

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed using a multidetector scanner following the administration of 90 mL of iohexol nonionic intravenous contrast at a rate of 3.0 mL/sec. Axial images were obtained through the chest, abdomen, and pelvis during the portal venous phase of enhancement. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformatted images were generated. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and bone windows were reviewed. Comparison is made to the prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed approximately 3 months ago (first restaging examination on cabozantinib), as well as to the baseline examination at initiation of cabozantinib therapy.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesions are identified. No new parenchymal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pleural-based abnormalities to suggest treatment-related pneumonitis. The lung parenchyma is clear aside from the known pulmonary nodules, which are discussed below.

Pulmonary Nodules (Target Lesions):
- Right lower lobe: A well-circumscribed, mildly spiculated pulmonary nodule in the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe measures 0.7 cm in longest axial dimension, unchanged from 0.7 cm on the prior examination and decreased from 0.8 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation. Density and morphology are stable.
- Left lower lobe: A small round pulmonary nodule in the lateral basal segment of the left lower lobe measures 0.7 cm, stable compared to 0.7 cm on the prior examination and from baseline on cabozantinib. No internal change in density.
- Right upper lobe: A tiny subsolid-appearing nodule in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe measures 0.3 cm, stable in size and character compared to prior examinations throughout the course of therapy.

No new pulmonary nodules are identified above the threshold of detection on the current examination. No nodule has enlarged since the prior study.

Pleura: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pleural thickening or pleural-based masses.

Mediastinum and Hila: The heart is normal in size. The pericardium is unremarkable. No pericardial effusion. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes by size criteria. A few scattered sub-centimeter pretracheal and right paratracheal nodes are present, not appreciably changed from the prior examination and felt to be reactive in nature. The thoracic aorta and great vessels are normal in caliber. No mediastinal mass.

Chest Wall and Bones: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions are identified in the visualized thoracic spine, ribs, clavicles, or sternum. No pathologic fractures. Mild degenerative changes in the lower thoracic spine.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth contours and homogeneous parenchymal enhancement throughout all phases of imaging. No focal hepatic lesion, mass, or abnormal enhancement is identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is absent (cholecystectomy noted from prior records). No perihepatic fluid collection.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No splenic lesion.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances normally without focal mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic stranding. No pancreatic lesion.

Adrenal Glands: The left adrenal gland appears unremarkable. The right adrenal gland is surgically absent, consistent with right adrenalectomy performed at the time of cytoreductive nephrectomy. No suspicious soft tissue nodularity in the right adrenal bed.

Kidneys: The right kidney is absent following prior right radical nephrectomy. The renal fossa demonstrates expected postsurgical changes with mature fibrotic stranding and no evidence of local recurrence. The left kidney demonstrates compensatory hypertrophy with preserved cortical thickness and normal parenchymal enhancement. No left renal mass or hydronephrosis. No left renal vein thrombosis.

Nephrectomy Bed / Retroperitoneum: The nephrectomy bed is clean without any discrete soft tissue nodularity or enhancing mass that would suggest local tumor recurrence. The retroperitoneum is evaluated as follows:

Retroperitoneal and Pelvic Nodal Disease (Dominant Sites of Measurable Disease):
- Aortocaval nodal conglomerate: A cluster of coalescent lymph nodes in the aortocaval region, previously measuring 2.6 cm at the most recent prior scan and 3.4 cm at baseline prior to cabozantinib initiation, now measures approximately 2.5 cm in greatest cross-sectional dimension. The overall configuration and conspicuity of this conglomerate appear similar to the prior examination, with no appreciable interval growth and possible very subtle continued decrease. The internal density of this nodal conglomerate is heterogeneous with a degree of central hypodensity suggesting treatment-related necrosis or fibrosis.
- Right common iliac nodal mass: A discrete rounded soft tissue nodal mass situated along the right common iliac vessels, measuring approximately 2.2 cm in greatest axial dimension. This was 2.3 cm on the preceding examination and 3.1 cm at the time cabozantinib was started. Dimensions are thus stable at this restaging and substantially reduced compared to the cabozantinib baseline. The margins of this node remain well-defined without invasion of adjacent structures.
- Right obturator node: An oval lymph node in the right obturator fossa measuring 1.3 cm in greatest transverse dimension, stable compared to 1.4 cm on the prior scan (and down from 1.8 cm at baseline).
- Right external iliac node: A mildly enlarged lymph node along the right external iliac chain measures 0.8 cm, stable compared to 0.9 cm at the first restaging examination on cabozantinib.

No new retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymphadenopathy is identified on this examination.

Bowel: The visualized small bowel and large bowel are unremarkable without obstruction, wall thickening, or mesenteric stranding. No free intraperitoneal air or fluid.

Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The inferior vena cava is patent. No new venous thrombosis identified. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery appear patent.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is adequately distended and demonstrates a smooth wall without focal thickening, filling defect, or mass.

Prostate: The prostate gland is mildly enlarged, consistent with benign prostatic hyperplasia, unchanged from prior examinations. No dominant nodule or extracapsular extension identified.

Pelvic Soft Tissues: No new pelvic floor soft tissue masses or abnormal pelvic lymphadenopathy beyond the previously described right common iliac, right external iliac, and right obturator nodal disease (addressed above).

Bones of the Pelvis: A small sclerotic focus in the right iliac bone, previously measuring approximately 0.5 cm on the prior CT examination and flagged as indeterminate, is again identified on the current examination. The lesion is unchanged in size, density, and morphology compared to the preceding scan. It does not demonstrate surrounding marrow edema, cortical destruction, or aggressive periosteal reaction. Given its stable and benign-appearing characteristics over the interval period, this is most consistent with a small bone island (enostosis) or focal degenerative sclerosis. No new osseous lesions are identified in the pelvis, sacrum, or proximal femora. No lytic lesions. No pathologic cortical disruption.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. STABLE DISEASE on second restaging examination during cabozantinib therapy. The known sites of metastatic retroperitoneal and pelvic lymphadenopathy — including the aortocaval nodal conglomerate, right common iliac nodal mass, right obturator node, and right external iliac node — are all essentially unchanged to marginally smaller compared to the prior examination performed approximately 3 months ago. Taken in the context of the trajectory since cabozantinib initiation, all of these nodal stations remain substantially reduced compared to their pre-treatment dimensions, reflecting durable nodal disease control on tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy.

2. The three known bilateral pulmonary metastases (right lower lobe, left lower lobe, and right upper lobe) are stable and at their nadir dimensions. No new pulmonary nodules identified.

3. No new hepatic, adrenal, or visceral peritoneal metastases.

4. No new mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.

5. The right iliac bone sclerotic focus identified on the preceding CT remains entirely stable in size and character. Further characterization with dedicated bone scintigraphy or correlation is warranted as clinically indicated.

6. No locoregional recurrence in the right nephrectomy bed.

7. Postoperative changes from right radical nephrectomy, adrenalectomy, and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection are stable and unremarkable.
